title: Flex Direction
content:
### Flex Direction

**flex-direction** defines the main axis direction.

---

### Values

| Value | Direction | Main Axis |
|-------|-----------|-----------|
| `row` | Left to right (default) | Horizontal |
| `row-reverse` | Right to left | Horizontal (reversed) |
| `column` | Top to bottom | Vertical |
| `column-reverse` | Bottom to top | Vertical (reversed) |

---

### Row (Default)

```css
.container {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row;
}
```

Items flow horizontally from left to right.
`justify-content` controls horizontal alignment.
`align-items` controls vertical alignment.

---

### Row-Reverse

```css
.container {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row-reverse;
}
```

Items flow right to left. Visual order is reversed.

---

### Column

```css
.container {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
```

Items stack top to bottom.
`justify-content` controls **vertical** alignment.
`align-items` controls **horizontal** alignment.

---

### Column-Reverse

```css
.container {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column-reverse;
}
```

Items stack bottom to top. Visual order reversed.

---

### When to Use Each

| Direction | Best For |
|-----------|----------|
| `row` | Navigation bars, button groups, cards in a row |
| `column` | Vertical lists, stacked cards, sidebars |
| `row-reverse` | RTL layouts, back-to-front ordering |
| `column-reverse` | Latest-first lists (timelines) |

---

### Impact on Properties

| In `row` | In `column` |
|----------|-------------|
| `justify-content` = horizontal | `justify-content` = vertical |
| `align-items` = vertical | `align-items` = horizontal |
| `width` matters | `height` matters |
| `flex-basis` = width | `flex-basis` = height |

> **Key Point:** Changing `flex-direction` swaps which axis is main vs cross. `justify-content` always works on the main axis.
codeExample:
flex-direction: row

1
2
3

flex-direction: column

1
2
3

quiz.question: In flex-direction: column, what does justify-content control?
